atoms.semantic_claim: Defines the Summit 7-field deposit event topic as keccak256("DepositEvent(bytes,bytes,bytes,bytes,bytes,bytes,bytes)") for Ed25519 node key plus BLS consensus key deposit events.

destinations.evidence: The destination port now exposes the same Summit deposit topic value from chainspec constants, matching the downstream atom at the original Reth location.

The destination dependency contains the same B256 topic value and signature string under the renamed constant SUMMIT_DEPOSIT_SIGNATURE, uses it to filter and decode Summit 7-field deposit logs, and has a unit test asserting the expected hash.
The port workspace points alloy-evm at the local ritual-alloy-evm-internal dependency where the Summit deposit topic behavior now lives.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Construct a receipt log at the configured deposit contract address whose first topic is 0x0cff1634a9a72df9d813ea7e3a3874a76086e081904ad3cf81e12e43f4d4c87a and whose data is ABI-encoded as the Summit 7-field event; parsing must accept it and produce the expected Summit deposit bytes, while changing only the topic to an unrelated B256 must not produce a deposit.
atoms.notes: The Reth port now has exact chainspec-level constant evidence; alloy-evm remains supporting execution-side evidence.

summary: The Summit deposit event topic constant is now present exactly in the Reth port chainspec constants file.
notes: Exact chainspec-level evidence was restored by adding SUMMIT_DEPOSIT_EVENT_TOPIC to crates/chainspec/src/constants.rs; alloy-evm still independently uses the same topic for EIP-6110 parsing.
